history.qwen_observation: The build failed at upgrade_build_to_java17 because the AspectJ Maven plugin (bundling AspectJ Compiler 1.9.2) does not recognize Java 17 source/target levels.
history.qwen_rationale: Insert an explicit step to upgrade aspectj-maven-plugin to 1.14.0 (which bundles AspectJ 1.9.22 and fully supports Java 17/21) before the Java 17 build step to resolve the compiler incompatibility.

history.qwen_observation: The build failed at upgrade_build_to_java17 because the AspectJ compiler (1.9.2) does not support Java 17 source levels, rejecting the -source 17 flag.
history.qwen_rationale: The previous attempt used UpgradePluginVersion, but the project likely manages the AspectJ version via a Maven property. I will explicitly change common AspectJ version properties to 1.9.22 (which supports Java 17/21) and bump the plugin to 1.14.0 before switching the JDK to 17. This ensures the AspectJ compiler invoked during the build is compatible with the new source level.

history.observation:
step `aspectj_bump` (jdk=11): BUILD FAILED post-recipe, rc=1
--- first error cluster ---
[08:37:57] PROJECT_ROOT=/work/src PHASE=build_post JDK=11
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal com.github.m50d:aspectj-maven-plugin:1.11.1:compile (default) on project powertac-aop: Execution default of goal com.github.m50d:aspectj-maven-plugin:1.11.1:compile failed: The AspectJ compiler needs at least Java runtime 17 -> [Help 1]
[ERROR] 
[ERROR] To see the full stack trace of the errors, re-run Maven with the -e switch.
[ERROR] Re-run Maven using the -X switch to enable full debug logging.
[ERROR] 
[ERROR] For more information about the errors and possible solutions, please read the following articles:
[ERROR] [Help 1] http://cwiki.apache.org/confluence/display/MAVEN/PluginExecutionException
[ERROR] 
[ERROR] After correcting the problems, you can resume the build with the command
[ERROR]   mvn <args> -rf :powertac-aop
history.qwen_observation: The build failed at `aspectj_bump` because AspectJ 1.9.22 requires a Java 17 runtime to execute, but the step was configured to run under JDK 11, and the active plugin artifact is `com.github.m50d:aspectj-maven-plugin` rather than `org.codehaus.mojo`.
history.qwen_rationale: Changing the execution JDK for the `aspectj_bump` step to 17 satisfies the AspectJ compiler's hard runtime requirement. Targeting the correct plugin artifact (`com.github.m50d`) ensures the version bump applies to the actual build configuration, allowing the compilation to proceed under the new JDK.
